Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KAMBIZ ADIBZADEH, ESQ. of Adibzadeh Law Firm, Tel No. 925-240-3240 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. MARK GOLDOWITZ, ESQ. of California Anti-Slapp Project, Tel No. 510-486-9123 appearing for Defendants Yelp! Inc. and Jeremy Stoppelman.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS YELP! INC. AND JEREMY STOPPELMAN'S Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is granted. The motion is timely filed based on the June 27, 2015 order lifting the stay. Even if the motion were not timely filed, there is good cause for the motion to be heard due to the uncertainty when the stay was lifted. The first prong is satisfied based on 425.16(e)(2), (e)(3) and (e)(4). The complaint is based on statements made in connections with an issue under consideration by a judicial body (amended complaint paragraph 24) which are not incidental to plaintiffs' claims. The complaint is also based on statements made in connection with an issue of public interest because matters of consumer protection are in the public interest. (Wilbanks v. Wolk (2004) 121 Cal. App. 4th 883, 900). As to the second prong, plaintiffs fail to show a possibility of prevailing on the merits of their claims because those claims are barred by 47 USC 230(c)(1). Plaintiffs' claims are based on how defendants edit and/or present reviews written by others. (Zeran v. America Online, Inc. (4th Cir. 1997) 129 F.3d 327, 330; Doe II v. MySpace Inc. (2009) 175 Cal. App. 4th 561, 570). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
